prompt:
Ticket from the facilities dashboard rewrite. Our door-controller event feed writes one JSON object per line, and the string encoding has burned us three times now, so I want our own encoder in `jsonquote.ts` with an exact, boring contract. `node --test test_jsonquote.ts` is acceptance; test file is read-only for you.

Why not JSON.stringify: (a) the old dashboard page inlines the newest lines into an inline <script> block, and a raw U+2028/U+2029 inside a string is a line terminator there — it has broken the page twice; stringify leaves those characters alone. (b) The door controllers occasionally truncate a UTF-16 buffer mid-character and hand us a lone surrogate; stringify emits it as an escaped \ud8xx sequence, which the collector's strict parser then rejects. I want those replaced outright.

`encodeJsonString(s: string): string` — returns the JSON string literal for `s`, surrounding double quotes included:
- `"` -> `\"` and backslash -> double backslash
- backspace, tab, LF, form feed, CR -> the short escapes `\b` `\t` `\n` `\f` `\r`
- every other code point below U+0020 -> `\uXXXX`, four lowercase hex digits
- U+2028 and U+2029 -> `\u2028` / `\u2029` (nothing else above U+001F gets hex-escaped)
- a lone surrogate (high with no low right after it, or low with no high right before) -> the replacement character U+FFFD, emitted literally
- everything else, including properly paired astral characters and accented text, passes through literally

`eventLine(fields: Record<string, string>): string` — one compact object line: `{` + `"key":"value"` pairs joined by commas + `}`, keys in default `Array.prototype.sort()` order, keys AND values both through encodeJsonString. Empty record gives `{}`. No spaces anywhere.

The tests pin expected output byte for byte — that's the point of the ticket. No deps, no package.json.
